recent_events_footnote: Source: Taiwan Ministry of National Defense daily report. Aircraft = PLA aircraft detected in Taiwan ADIZ; Vessels = PLA naval vessels detected near Taiwan.

map.transponder_note: Transponders usually off; see MND data for counts

methodology_overview: The Threat Index is a composite score (0–100) combining military activity signals from Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense with SAR satellite port monitoring. It uses statistical anomaly detection against a 30-day rolling baseline.
methodology_aircraft: Standard deviation from 30-day mean. ≤1σ = 0, linear 1σ–3σ, capped at 25
methodology_centerline: Tiered by total aircraft count. Low totals cap the max score even at 100% crossing rate
methodology_vessels: Same σ-based anomaly as aircraft, against 30-day mean
methodology_port_surge: Multiple ports with σ≥2 vessel count (buildup): 1 port = 3, 2 = 6, 3 = 10, ≥4 = 15
methodology_port_departure: Multiple ports with σ≤-2 vessel count (deployed): same scale, max 15
methodology_source: Data sources: Taiwan MND daily reports (radar), OpenSky Network (ADS-B), Sentinel-1 SAR satellite imagery. Methodology inspired by Caldara-Iacoviello GPR Index.

satellite.no_data: No satellite data available yet. The SAR processor runs every 12 hours.
